name: EOQ Inventory Calculator
description: Industrial-grade Economic Order Quantity calculator with integrated cost analysis, safety stock, and reorder point optimization. Compliant with ISO 9001, Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC standards.

inputs.label: Annual Demand (units)
inputs.businessContext: Total forecasted demand for the item over one year. Used to calculate order frequency and total cycle stock.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total forecasted demand for the item over one year. Used to calculate order frequency and total cycle stock.

inputs.label: Ordering Cost per Order
inputs.businessContext: Fixed cost incurred per purchase order (e.g., administrative, inspection, transportation setup).

businessContext_i18n.en: Fixed cost incurred per purchase order (e.g., administrative, inspection, transportation setup).

inputs.label: Holding Cost per Unit per Year
inputs.businessContext: Cost to hold one unit in inventory for one year, including storage, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost.

label_i18n.en: Holding Cost per Unit per Year

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost to hold one unit in inventory for one year, including storage, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost.

inputs.label: Unit Cost (Purchase Price)
inputs.businessContext: Purchase price per unit from supplier. Used to calculate total annual purchase cost and capital tied up.

businessContext_i18n.en: Purchase price per unit from supplier. Used to calculate total annual purchase cost and capital tied up.

inputs.label: Lead Time (days)
inputs.businessContext: Average time from order placement to receipt. Critical for safety stock and reorder point calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average time from order placement to receipt. Critical for safety stock and reorder point calculations.

inputs.label: Demand Variability (Std Dev of Daily Demand)
inputs.businessContext: Standard deviation of daily demand. Used to calculate safety stock under uncertainty (Six Sigma approach).

label_i18n.en: Demand Variability (Std Dev of Daily Demand)

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard deviation of daily demand. Used to calculate safety stock under uncertainty (Six Sigma approach).

inputs.label: Desired Service Level
inputs.businessContext: Target probability of not stocking out during lead time. Higher service levels increase safety stock.

businessContext_i18n.en: Target probability of not stocking out during lead time. Higher service levels increase safety stock.

inputs.label: Backorder Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Cost incurred per unit when demand cannot be met from stock (lost sales, penalty, expediting).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost incurred per unit when demand cannot be met from stock (lost sales, penalty, expediting).

inputs.label: Warehouse Capacity (units)
inputs.businessContext: Maximum storage capacity available for this item. Used to constrain order quantity if EOQ exceeds capacity.

businessContext_i18n.en: Maximum storage capacity available for this item. Used to constrain order quantity if EOQ exceeds capacity.

inputs.label: Storage Cost per sq ft per Year
inputs.businessContext: Cost per square foot of warehouse space per year. Used for space-based holding cost adjustments.

label_i18n.en: Storage Cost per sq ft per Year

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per square foot of warehouse space per year. Used for space-based holding cost adjustments.

inputs.label: Item Footprint (sq ft per unit)
inputs.businessContext: Average floor space required per unit of inventory. Used to compute space cost component.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average floor space required per unit of inventory. Used to compute space cost component.

rules.message: Annual demand must be greater than zero.
Ordering cost, holding cost, and unit cost must be positive.
Service level must be one of the predefined values.
EOQ exceeds warehouse capacity. Consider reducing order quantity or increasing capacity.

thresholds.message: Total inventory cost exceeds 20% of total purchase cost. Review holding or ordering costs.
Safety stock is more than 50% of EOQ. High demand variability or service level may be driving inefficiency.
Inventory turns are below 4. Consider reducing order quantity or increasing demand.
Space cost is more than 30% of total holding cost. Evaluate warehouse layout or item footprint.

formulas.name: Economic Order Quantity
formulas.description: Classic EOQ formula minimizing total ordering and holding costs.

formulas.name: Safety Stock
formulas.description: Safety stock based on demand variability and service level (Z-score).

formulas.name: Reorder Point
formulas.description: Reorder point considering lead time demand and safety stock.

formulas.name: Total Annual Holding Cost
formulas.description: Annual cost of holding inventory (cycle stock + safety stock).

formulas.name: Space Cost Component
formulas.description: Annual cost of warehouse space occupied by inventory.

formulas.name: Total Annual Inventory Cost
formulas.description: Sum of ordering, holding, and space costs (primary result).

primary.label: Total Annual Inventory Cost
primary.description: The primary result: total cost of ordering, holding, and space for the optimal EOQ policy.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excess Ordering Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If ordering cost is high relative to holding cost, consider consolidating orders or using blanket POs.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Demand Uncertainty Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: High demand variability inflates safety stock and holding costs. Consider demand smoothing or VMI.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Space Utilization Inefficiency
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Item footprint or storage cost per sqft is high relative to unit cost. Evaluate storage method or item design.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Ordering Cost
suggestedActions.description: Implement e-procurement or blanket orders to lower ordering cost, which will reduce EOQ and total cost.

suggestedActions.label: Negotiate Storage or Insurance
suggestedActions.description: Review warehouse contracts or insurance premiums to lower holding cost per unit.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Demand Forecasting
suggestedActions.description: Use statistical forecasting or AI to reduce demand variability, lowering safety stock.

suggestedActions.label: Review Service Level Target
suggestedActions.description: If backorder cost is low, consider reducing service level to cut safety stock.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Warehouse Layout
suggestedActions.description: Reduce item footprint by using vertical storage or better racking systems.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Total Cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total annual inventory cost adjusted by a confidence factor (0.8 to 1.2) based on input data quality (e.g., variability, historical accuracy). Default 1.0 if all inputs are within typical ranges.
